incorrects.description: Student placed 4 in groups slot but wrong value in items slot on first attempt

steps.text: You got the groups. Now count how many dots are in one circle.

incorrects.description: Student placed 4 in items slot but wrong value in groups slot on first attempt

steps.text: You got the dots in each. Now count how many circles there are.

incorrects.description: Student placed wrong values in both slots on first attempt

steps.text: Let's try again. How many circles? How many dots in each circle?

incorrects.description: Student placed 4 in groups slot but wrong value in items slot on second attempt

steps.text: The first number is right. Look inside one circle. Count the dots you see. That number goes in the second slot.

incorrects.description: Student placed 4 in items slot but wrong value in groups slot on second attempt

steps.text: The second number is right. Now count the circles themselves. How many groups do you see? That number goes in the first slot.

incorrects.description: Student placed wrong values in both slots on second attempt

params.description: Circles highlight one by one, then dots in one circle highlight.

steps.text: Here's what helps. Count the circles first. That's how many groups. Then look inside one circle and count the dots. That's how many in each.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: System highlights circles sequentially (1, 2, 3, 4), then highlights dots in one circle (1, 2, 3, 4).
System places 4 tile in first slot.
System places 4 tile in second slot.

steps.text: Let me show you. I count the circles: 1, 2, 3, 4. Four groups. I place the 4 tile first. Then I count dots in one circle: 1, 2, 3, 4. Four in each. I place the 4 tile second. The expression is 4 times 4. That shows 4 groups of 4.
